steps.description: Use the keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Shift+P in Visual Studio Code to open the Command Palette, enabling access to various commands within the context of the "MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT" interface.
Type 'view: show run and debug' into the VS Code Command Palette input field to initiate the 'View: Show Run and Debug' command.
Press the Enter key to select "View: Show Run and Debug" from the command palette in Visual Studio Code.
Click the green "Start Debugging (F5)" button in the Run and Debug panel of Visual Studio Code, located at the top left of the interface.

@assertion debug select ordered as same as the follow:
Debug in Copilot (Edge)
Debug in Copilot (Chrome)
Preview in Copilot (Edge)

Preview in Copilot (Chrome)
Click the "Run and Debug" icon in the left activity bar of the Visual Studio Code interface to open the run/debug panel.
Press the F1 key to open the Command Palette.
Type #text:'Debug: Select and Start Debugging' into the VS Code Command Palette input.
Press Enter to confirm the selection of "Debug: Select and Start Debugging" in the VS Code command palette dropdown.
Type "Debug in Copilot (Chrome)" into the VS Code run/debug configuration dropdown at the top center of the application window.
Press Enter to select "Debug in Copilot (Chrome)" from the configuration dropdown in the Visual Studio Code debug panel.
Click on the "Email or phone" input field within the Microsoft Sign-In page to focus the cursor for user input.
Type ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}} into the email or username input field on the Microsoft login page.
Press the "Enter" key to submit the email input on the Microsoft account "Sign in" page.
Type  ${{secret:M365_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD}} into the password input field on the Microsoft login page to authenticate user  ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}}
Press the "Enter" key to submit the password field in the Microsoft login page.
Press the "Enter" key to confirm the "Stay signed in?" prompt by selecting the highlighted "Yes" button on the Microsoft login page in a browser.
Use keyboard shortcut "Ctrl+-" to zoom out on the "Chat | M365 Copilot" page in a web browser.
Press the F5 key to refresh the M365 Copilot chat interface within the Microsoft Edge browser, reloading the page displaying the "Hi, what can I help you with?" prompt and sidebar agent list.
@assertion ${{var:app_name}}local displayed on the main section of the screen.
